agent_output: {"handoff_brief":{"recipient":"Shaq","task_context":"Implement a pure TypeScript function `recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number` that applies bulk discount logic and enforces a minimum price floor. This function will be called inline from a high-traffic pricing endpoint (~500 req/sec peak), so it must be stateless, deterministic, and have zero external dependencies. Deliver the function plus a unit test suite.","domain_rules":[{"rule":"Final price = base price minus applicable discounts, floored at $0.01. Price can never be zero or negative.","testable_assertion":"For any inputs, recalculatePrice(...) >= 0.01"},{"rule":"Bulk discount: 10% off base price when quantity >= 10.","testable_assertion":"recalculatePrice(10, 100) === 90 AND recalculatePrice(9, 100) === 100"}],"architecture_guidance":[{"decision":"Pure TypeScript function, no classes or external dependencies","implementation_note":"Signature: recalculatePrice(quantity: number, basePrice: number): number. Stateless and deterministic — no side effects, no I/O."},{"decision":"Add JSDoc documenting the $0.01 floor behavior","implementation_note":"On-call engineers need to understand why a price of $0.01 can appear in production. Document the floor rule and the condition that triggers it directly above the function."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"criterion":"Bulk discount applied at quantity threshold","given":"quantity=10, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 4.50"},{"criterion":"No discount below threshold","given":"quantity=9, basePrice=$5.00","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 5.00"},{"criterion":"Price floor enforced","given":"a discount that would reduce price to $0.00 or below","when":"recalculatePrice is called","then":"returns 0.01"},{"criterion":"Unit test suite covers all acceptance criteria plus edge cases","given":"the implemented function","when":"tests are run","then":"all pass, covering: threshold boundary (qty 9 vs 10), floor activation, typical cases, and zero/negative basePrice if applicable"}],"terminology_alignment":[{"domain_term":"bulk discount","technical_term":"10% multiplier when quantity >= 10","definition":"A flat 10% reduction off base price, applied when the order quantity meets or exceeds 10 units."},{"domain_term":"price floor","technical_term":"Math.max(computedPrice, 0.01)","definition":"The minimum allowable final price is $0.01. Prevents zero or negative prices from propagating downstream."}],"contradictions_resolved":[],"open_questions":["Should basePrice <= 0 be treated as an error (throw) or clamped to the $0.01 floor? Bird's rules don't cover invalid inputs. Recommend: throw on basePrice <= 0 since a non-positive base price is a data bug, not a discount scenario. Shaq should implement this guard and flag if Bird disagrees.","Is the 10% discount always on base price, or could future discount types stack? Current spec says single rule. Implement for single rule now but keep the discount calculation isolated so stacking can be added later."]},"escalations":[],"confidence":{"level":92,"high_confidence_areas":["Domain rules are unambiguous and all three agents are aligned","Architecture is simple — pure function, no moving parts","Acceptance criteria are concrete with exact expected values"],"low_confidence_areas":["Handling of invalid inputs (negative or zero basePrice) is unspecified by Bird","Future extensibility for multiple discount types is unclear"],"assumptions":["Discount applies to base price only (not recursively)","Currency precision is standard two decimal places (no sub-cent rounding issues beyond the floor)","The $0.01 floor applies after all discounts, not between discount steps"]}}
